methodsAttempted.notes: Primary domain: columbia.edu. Key subdomains: provost.columbia.edu, ai.ctl.columbia.edu, www.it.cuimc.columbia.edu, students.business.columbia.edu, www.law.columbia.edu, www.tc.columbia.edu, intranet.sps.columbia.edu, etc.cuit.columbia.edu
OpenCLI Google search. Found: Provost Generative AI Policy, CBS student policy, Law School interim policy, Provost AI resources hub, CUIMC IT guidance, TC guidance, SPS guidance.
OpenCLI Google search. Found: CTL AI tools learner perspectives, CUIT ChatGPT education page, CBS academic integrity, CS department article.
OpenCLI Google search. Found: Columbia University AI Guidelines hub (ai.ctl.columbia.edu), Columbia Spectator article, VerifyWise policy library reference.
Cloudflare WAF blocks HTTP fetch (403). Search results confirm: /content/office-senior-vice-provost/ai-policy (Generative AI Policy) and /content/generative-ai (AI resources hub).
Verified: /explore/columbia-guidelines/ (HTTP 200, readable). Links to Provost policy, academic integrity, CUIT resources, best practices.

provost.columbia.edu blocked by Cloudflare WAF. ai.ctl.columbia.edu accessible. www.it.cuimc.columbia.edu accessible. www.law.columbia.edu accessible. students.business.columbia.edu blocked by Cloudflare.

summary: Columbia has a comprehensive university-wide Generative AI Policy from the Office of the Provost, plus departmental implementations. Key sources: (1) Provost Generative AI Policy (provost.columbia.edu, Cloudflare-protected, needs browser); (2) Columbia University AI Guidelines hub (ai.ctl.columbia.edu, accessible); (3) CUIMC IT AI guidance (accessible); (4) Law School interim policy (accessible); (5) CBS student policy, TC guidance, SPS guidance (various access levels). The Provost policy is the primary university-wide source but requires browser access. ai.ctl.columbia.edu serves as the accessible hub linking to all Columbia AI resources.
